critique.summary: The article appears to be a collection of links and resources related to various media types, including audio, images, software, and texts, primarily from the Internet Archive. It does not present a cohesive narrative or analysis.
mainPoints: The article lists various categories of media available on the Internet Archive.
It includes sections for audio, images, software, texts, and video.
critique.truthfulness: The article does not contain specific claims that can be verified for accuracy, as it primarily serves as a directory of resources. However, the information about the Internet Archive is generally accurate.
considerations: The article lacks context or explanation about the significance of the resources listed.
There are no citations or sources provided to support the claims about the Internet Archive.

comments_critique.summary: The discussion revolves around the functionality and performance of a physics simulation in a web application. Users share their experiences, issues, and nostalgic references related to the implementation.

comments.author: jaen
comments.text: What's the physics solver used here? This demonstration quickly runs into common problems associated with overly simplistic solvers/integrators where everything starts to become unstable and "vibrate" / "twitch".
comments.analysis: This comment provides a critical analysis of the underlying physics, highlighting potential issues with the implementation.

comments.author: jp57
comments.text: Because there are no instructions at all about what will happen when you click, I almost immediately created a "transporter accident" where I spawned in a new shape that intersected with an existing shape.  They became locked together, and this new compound shape seems to exist as some kind of physics engine edge case.  It struggled madly to find a low-energy state, rolling and jittering around, sometimes seeming to almost stop, and then gain energy again from nowhere and start moving again.
comments.analysis: This comment describes a specific experience with the application, offering insight into user interaction and potential design flaws.

comments.author: Tepix
comments.text: It seems to be broken on Firefox. It shows 3/8 players, the circles are flickering madly and I can't drag them.
comments.analysis: While the comment points out a compatibility issue, it lacks detail on the broader implications of the problem.

comments.author: NewKrok
comments.text: Wow, didn't expect to see my implementation here :)
comments.analysis: This comment is more of a personal reaction and does not contribute to the technical discussion.

comments.author: coldcity_again
comments.text:
Bit twitchy indeed but still fun. Instantly took me back to "rubber glenz vectors" in classic Amiga prods[1]

[1] https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OZTnR3FpUEA&start=413
comments.analysis: While nostalgic, this comment does not provide substantial technical feedback or analysis of the application.
